title: Vouacapoua fraxinifolia
taxonomy: Vouacapoua fraxinifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is classified as a legume, which is a type of plant that produces seeds in pods. Within the Fabaceae family, Vouacapoua fraxinifolia is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae. As a member of this subfamily, it shares characteristics with other plants that have compound leaves and produce flowers in inflorescences.

The health benefits of Vouacapoua fraxinifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
